records.ety: [F. escadron, formerly also esquadron, Sp. escuadron, or It. squadrone. See Squad.]

defs: Primarily, a square; hence, a square body of troops; a body of troops drawn up in a square.
quotes: Those half-rounding quards Just met, and, closing, stood in squadron joined.

defs: A body of cavalry comprising two companies or troops, and averaging from one hundred and twenty to two hundred men.
A detachment of vessels employed on any particular service or station, under the command of the senior officer; as, the North Atlantic Squadron.
